title: What TekAutomate Is
body:
TekAutomate is an automation authoring environment for Tektronix and Keithley test & measurement instruments.
Users build workflows that control oscilloscopes, power supplies, SMUs, DMMs, AFGs, and TekExpress applications, then export runnable Python scripts.
Delivery: Web app (http://dev.tek.com/TekAutomate); optional Electron desktop packaging.
Stack: React, TypeScript, Google Blockly, CodeMirror. Python is generated entirely client-side — no backend server.
Users: Test/validation engineers, R&D, researchers, educators, AEs.

title: Two Authoring Modes: Steps Builder vs Blockly Builder
body:
Steps Builder: Linear step list, command library browser, single-device only. Exports legacy Python.
Blockly Builder: Drag-and-drop blocks, loops, variables, full multi-device support. Exports clean Python.
Workflows convert bidirectionally: Steps → Blockly via stepToBlock.ts, Blockly → Steps via blockToStep.ts.
RULE: For multi-device workflows, always use Blockly Builder. Steps UI cannot generate correct multi-device Python.

title: Connection Types and VISA Resource Strings
body:
TCP/IP (VXI-11): TCPIP::<host>::INSTR — no port needed, uses RPC portmapper.
Socket: TCPIP::<host>::<port>::SOCKET — raw socket, default port 4000. NOT supported with tm_devices backend.
USB: USB::<vendor>::<product>::<serial>::INSTR — e.g. USB::0x0699::0x0522::INSTR
GPIB: GPIB<board>::<address>::INSTR — address 1-30, e.g. GPIB0::1::INSTR
Socket connection is NOT supported with tm_devices backend — use TCP/IP instead.

title: All Valid Step Types in Steps Builder
body:
connect: Opens device connections. Key params: instrumentIds[], printIdn.
write: SCPI write (no return). Key param: command.
query: SCPI query (returns value). Key params: command, saveAs (REQUIRED).
set_and_query: Write then verify readback. Key params: command, queryCommand, saveAs.
sleep: Wait N seconds. Key param: duration.
python: Custom inline Python code. Key param: code.
save_waveform: Waveform save. Key params: source, filename, format.
save_screenshot: Capture screenshot. Key params: filename, scopeType (modern|legacy).
recall: Recall waveform/setup. Key params: recallType, filePath, reference.
sweep: Parameter sweep loop. Key params: variableName, start, stop, step, saveResults.
error_check: Validate response. Key params: command, expectedValue.
comment: Annotation only, no code generated. Key param: text.
group: Container for nested steps. Key params: children[], instrumentAlias.
disconnect: Close all connections.

title: Query Steps MUST Have saveAs Parameter
body:
Every query step MUST have a saveAs (or outputVariable) parameter. This is REQUIRED.
Without saveAs, the query result is not stored and cannot be used in subsequent steps.
Correct: { type: 'query', params: { command: 'CH1:SCALE?', saveAs: 'scale_value' } }
Wrong:   { type: 'query', params: { command: 'CH1:SCALE?' } }  ← Missing saveAs, will fail.

title: Device Binding Rules and Resolution Order
body:
Steps bind to devices via boundDeviceId. Commands reference devices by instrumentAlias or instrumentId.
Resolution order (highest to lowest priority):
1. Command-level instrumentAlias
2. Group-level instrumentAlias  
3. Default device (first enabled device in DeviceEntry list)
In generated Python: each device alias becomes a Python variable (scope, psu, dmm, smu).

title: Supported Backends Overview
body:
PyVISA (pyvisa): Industry standard. TCP/IP, Socket, USB, GPIB. All devices. pip install pyvisa.
tm_devices (tm_devices): Official Tektronix framework. TCP/IP, USB, GPIB. NO Socket support. pip install tm-devices.
VXI-11 (vxi11): Lightweight, no VISA drivers. TCP/IP only. All devices. pip install vxi11.
TekHSI (tekhsi): High-speed gRPC. TCP/IP port 5000 only. MSO5/6, DPO7K only. pip install tekhsi.
Hybrid: NOT a fifth backend. It is a mode where one workflow uses PyVISA (SCPI) + TekHSI (waveforms) simultaneously.
Socket connection: ONLY supported by PyVISA. tm_devices, VXI-11, TekHSI do NOT support Socket.

title: tm_devices Backend Restrictions — CRITICAL
body:
tm_devices backend FORBIDS these step types:
  ❌ scpi_write / write — Use tm_devices API blocks instead (e.g. scope.commands.acquire.state.write('RUN'))
  ❌ scpi_query / query — Use tm_devices API blocks instead
  ❌ save_screenshot — Use scope.save_screenshot(name) method
  ❌ save_waveform — Switch backend to PyVISA or use scope.curve_query()
Exception: python_code blocks bypass validation — raw scope.write() is allowed inside python_code even with tm_devices.
When violated, generator aborts with: BACKEND CAPABILITY VIOLATION DETECTED.

title: Hybrid Mode — Not a Fifth Backend
body:
Hybrid is NOT a fifth backend. It is a mode where a single workflow uses more than one backend per device.
Typically: SCPI commands via PyVISA + waveform capture via TekHSI.
Generated code uses two connection objects for the same device:
  scpi = rm.open_resource('TCPIP::192.168.1.10::INSTR')  # PyVISA for SCPI
  scope = TekHSIConnect('192.168.1.10:5000')              # TekHSI for waveforms
Supported devices: MSO5/6, DPO7K only.

title: Backend Selection Decision Tree
body:
FastFrame/FastAcq waveform capture ONLY → TekHSI
Measurements/search/histogram → PyVISA (NOT TekHSI)
Modern Python API on MSO6B → tm_devices
TekExpress compliance testing → PyVISA SOCKET (port 5000)
Unsure or maximum compatibility → PyVISA
DPO70k or older scope → PyVISA
Linux without VISA drivers → VXI-11 or PyVISA-py
Multi-device management → tm_devices
TekscopePC → tm_devices with HOST=127.0.0.1

title: Python Code Generation Pipeline
body:
Steps UI or Blockly workspace → Converter/Generator → Analyze devices → determine backends → resolve imports
→ Generate: imports → connections → command body → cleanup → Downloadable .py file.
Key files:
  src/App.tsx — Steps UI Python generator
  src/components/BlocklyBuilder/generators/pythonGenerators.ts — Blockly Python generator
  src/components/BlocklyBuilder/converters/blockToStep.ts — Blockly→Steps
  src/components/BlocklyBuilder/converters/stepToBlock.ts — Steps→Blockly

title: OPC Query Rules — Critical
body:
NEVER use query('*OPC?') after FILESYSTEM:READFILE + read_raw(). This causes UnicodeDecodeError.
The PNG binary data contaminates the read buffer. The subsequent *OPC? reads leftover binary bytes.
Fix: Use time.sleep(1.0) instead of *OPC? after all binary file transfer operations.
For tm_devices: use scope.commands.opc.query().strip() == '1' (string comparison, not int).
For PyVISA: result = device.query('*OPC?').strip() — always .strip() before comparison.

title: FILESystem:READFile — Raw Socket Required
body:
FILESystem:READFile is a command (not a query) that produces unframed binary output.
It omits IEEE 488.2 block headers and is terminated only by EOI.
VISA-based APIs (PyVISA, tm_devices) CANNOT reliably consume this output.
Raw TCP socket access with timeout-based EOF detection is REQUIRED.
After sending FILESystem:READFile: do NOT query anything, do NOT send other SCPI commands.
Let the socket stay in raw streaming mode until timeout. This is the One-Shot Binary Window Rule.

title: TekscopePC-Specific Rules
body:
Connection: Use tm_devices backend with HOST=127.0.0.1.
Reset: Use scpi_write('*RST') NOT scope.reset() — scope.reset() causes AttributeError with PyVISA.
Measurements: Must query explicitly — no SAVE:MEASUREMENT:ALL command.
FastFrame: Standard commands work normally.

title: Screenshot Capture Rules by Scope Family
body:
MSO 4/5/6 Series (modern):
  Use SAVE:IMAGe "C:/Temp/screenshot.png" — format comes from file extension.
  Do NOT use EXPort or HARDCopy commands on MSO 4/5/6.
  After capture: time.sleep(1.0) then FILESystem:READFile via raw socket.

MSO/DPO 70000 Series (legacy):
  Use EXPort:FILEName + EXPort:FORMat PNG + EXPort START (case sensitive: START not STARt).
  Do NOT use SAVE:IMAGe on 70000 series.
  After capture: time.sleep(1.0) then FILESystem:READFile via raw socket.

NEVER use *OPC? after file transfer. Use time.sleep(1.0) instead.

title: Blockly controls_for MUST Include mutation/variable
body:
controls_for (for loop) blocks MUST include the mutation element with variable name.
Without mutation, Blockly variable scoping breaks and round-trip import/export is unstable.
CORRECT XML:
  <block type="controls_for" id="loop1">
    <field name="VAR">frame</field>
    <mutation><variable>frame</variable></mutation>
  </block>
WRONG XML (missing mutation):
  <block type="controls_for" id="loop1">
    <field name="VAR">frame</field>
  </block>

title: Blockly DEVICE_CONTEXT Field Rules
body:
DEVICE_CONTEXT field on SCPI blocks determines which instrument gets the command.
Resolution priority:
1. Explicit DEVICE_CONTEXT field on block (absolute priority — return immediately)
2. Walk getPreviousBlock() chain to find nearest connect_to_instrument
3. Walk parent blocks (for blocks inside loops/conditions)
4. Fall back to currentDeviceContext module variable

Command prefix → DEVICE_CONTEXT mapping:
  CH1:|ACQuire:|MEASU:|DATa: → (scope)
  :SOURce:|:OUTPut:|:MEASure: → (smu)/(psu)
  TEKEXP: → (tekexp)

Common GPT error: Scope commands (CH1:SCAle) given SMU device context.

title: Blockly Variable Name Resolution — Critical Fix
body:
PROBLEM: block.getFieldValue('VAR') returns Blockly's internal UUID, not the human-readable name.
This causes Python output like: N%hHHhp:Bg=T0!BWtnrB = 0 instead of i = 0.
FIX: Always use workspace API to resolve variable names:
  const varModel = workspace.getVariableById(id);
  const varName = varModel.getName();  // 'voltage', not 'abc-123-def'
Applied at 3 locations in blockToStep.ts.

title: Float Loop Must Use while, Not range()
body:
Python's range() does not accept float arguments.
When loop start/stop/step are floats, generator MUST produce a while loop, not for i in range().
CORRECT (float values):
  v = 0.5
  while v <= 2.5:
      # loop body
      v += 0.5
WRONG (causes TypeError):
  for v in range(0.5, 2.5, 0.5):  ← Python range() rejects floats

title: AiAction Schema — Do Not Break This Contract
body:
AiAction interface (must remain unchanged for apply-engine compatibility):
  type: 'replace_command' | 'insert_step' | 'delete_step' | 'change_param' | 'change_backend' | 'change_device' | 'reorder'
  targetStepId: string | null  — step ID this action targets (null for flow-level)
  suggestedFix: string         — human-readable description of the fix
  severity: 'critical' | 'warning' | 'info'
  payload?: Record<string, unknown>  — optional: concrete new value (command text, param value, etc.)
onApplyAiActions is the ONLY mutation path for AI-suggested fixes. It lives in App.tsx.

title: AiFinding Schema
body:
AiFinding interface:
  symptom: string  — human-readable symptom description
  affectedStepId: string | null  — step ID (null for flow-level findings)
  category: 'scpi_error' | 'timeout' | 'device_mismatch' | 'missing_step' | 'parameter_issue' | 'backend_conflict' | 'performance' | 'general'
  confidence: number  — 0-1 confidence that this finding is correct

title: TekExpress Integration Rules
body:
TekExpress commands are SCPI strings sent over PyVISA SOCKET backend. NEVER raw socket.sendall().
Connection: TCPIP::localhost::5000::SOCKET with write_termination='
' read_termination='
' timeout=30000.
Synchronization: Use TEKEXP:STATE? polling, NOT *OPC? (*OPC? not supported by TekExpress).
Execution is async/state-machine — must poll TEKEXP:STATE? until COMPLETE.
Handle popups: query TEKEXP:POPUP? and respond with TEKEXP:POPUP 'OK'.
Backends: ONLY PyVISA SOCKET supported. tm_devices, TekHSI, VXI-11 all unsupported for TekExpress.

title: SCPI Command JSON Schema
body:
Each command in public/commands/*.json has these fields:
  id, category, scpi, header, commandType (set|query|both)
  shortDescription, description, mnemonics[]
  instruments: { families: [], models: [] }
  arguments: [{ name, type, validValues }]
  syntax: { set, query }
  codeExamples: [{ scpi: {code}, python: {code}, tm_devices: {code} }]
Query vs Write detection: ends with ? OR type='query' → query; no ? OR type='write' → write.
Parameter substitution: CH${channel}:SCALE ${scale} with values → CH2:SCALE 0.5

title: Multi-Device Workflow Rules
body:
Steps UI is single-device only. For multi-device: always use Blockly Builder.
Each device gets its own Python variable based on its alias: scope, psu, dmm, smu, awg, afg, sa.
Connect first, disconnect last — every workflow must begin with connect and end with disconnect.
IP Conflict Detection: generator aborts if multiple devices share same IP address.
Cleanup must close ALL connected devices, not just the first one.
